prompt: A highly realistic documentary-style photo of a rural Chinese primary school gate-side snack shop in 1998, shot in warm afternoon sunlight. The scene shows an old earthen-walled village storefront with cracked yellow-brown plaster, exposed brick edges, a traditional tiled roof with visible wooden rafters, and two weathered wooden window shutters opened outward to form a sales window. In front of the window is a simple glass display case filled with school supplies and small books. A middle-aged female shopkeeper sits on a low wooden stool beside the case, wearing a faded blue jacket, black pants, and black cloth shoes, handling stationery or a metal cash box. Four primary school boys stand in a short line at the counter with their backs and side profiles to the camera, wearing late-1990s Chinese school tracksuits and bright red Young Pioneer scarves; two wear red-and-white jackets with blue pants, and two wear blue-and-white jackets with blue pants. Inside the window shelves are densely stocked with period-appropriate goods: multiple rows of old-fashioned glass bottled sodas and soft drinks on the top shelf, colorful fruit candies and gumballs in clear plastic jars, small snack packets, instant treats, and boxed sweets on the middle shelves, and at the counter many pencils, rulers, notebooks, exercise books, and children's stationery displayed upright. Add a faded red cloth hanging from the left side of the window frame, dusty ground, a long mud wall receding into the background, and large leafy trees casting soft shadows. The composition should feel candid, historically believable, and slightly nostalgic, like an authentic photograph taken outside a countryside elementary school in northern China in the late 1990s. Vertical framing, eye-level perspective, natural colors, detailed textures, no stylization, no cinematic exaggeration, strong realism.
